I think Mandy quoted me in the inner circle group today, from the last inner circle. The more I learned about this whole business, it's all about belief. That's it. For you, it's belief in yourself. For them, it's belief in this thing that you're trying to create. So how do we create belief? A story for me. So I'm calling this one, How to Break the Four-Minute Mile. So you guys know the story about four-minute mile? Roger Bannister in 1954, was the first dude who ever broke four-minute mile, and after that, a whole bunch of people have done it. But nobody thought it was possible. Like human beings cannot break it. And as soon as someone did it, it's like, oh, I believe it's possible, and then they can go and they can actually do it. Same thing happened to me. So August 17th, 2004. So that was, and what, 12, 13 years ago-ish? I was just getting into this whole business. My goal when I got started was, if I can make $1,000 a month, I'll be rich. That's it. If I had $1,000 a month, that would do everything. Because I think my wife and I just got married. Our mortgage was $4.75 a month for our little duplex for renting. We had a little car payment. So like $1,000 to cover all of our hard costs. And then like, what am I, my wife made, she's making $9.50 an hour. I'm like, that's like, we could go blow it on like dates and like go bowling and stuff, and that'd be awesome. So that's why I got in this whole business was like $1,000 a month. That was my whole goal. So I'm doing the whole thing, I'm learning, I'm having fun, I'm selling stuff on eBay, and Craigslist, and doing all sorts of stuff. Having a good time. And then about that time, there was this guy that was coming out, and everyone started kind of whispering and talking about this guy named John Reese. And I don't know if that was the kind of research in my case, this guy, and that's about all I knew. And then he started doing this pre-launch for this course called Traffic Secrets. He's like, we're doing this course, we're gonna launch it and all this stuff. And I go, he has to kind of fun to watch and see the model of what he's doing. So he went out there and he goes and he does this big launch. And when the launch happened, I actually was going on vacation with my family down to a place called Bear Lake, which is like five hours south of here. This couple of you guys have been to Bear Lake sounds like, which is like the coolest little town. And back then, 13 years ago, there was no internet in the city at all. And so we got there and there's no internet. And so me, I had my little brand new business thing, I had like three customers and I'm like stressing like, what if one of them emails me and they're gonna be pissed? I'm like refund the $17 I got. Like I was all panicking, right? So I'm sitting there on Bear Lake for like three or four days just all nervous and stuff. And finally I'm like, I gotta get internet. And they, we're asking everyone, finally someone's like, well, there's a library, if you go there, they've got dial-up modems and you can actually jump on them. Like, Colette, I have to go, like, kiss my customer's email and they can't like, I need to go. So go to the library, I go in there and I come in and there's like super quiet, you know, the library's all quiet, nush us. And there's two computers and there's like two chairs and there's a line of like three or four people behind each one. I'm like, oh, so stand in the line. I'm like waiting. And it's like, I think it's like a 10 minute limit. And then the person gets off, the next person gets in and finally get closer. And it's my time to say jump on the computer. And I start looking through my emails, I'm going through, my email address at the time was moneyforcollege at Juneau.com. So I go to Juneau, I log in and I see my emails in there. I'm looking through my emails and like, none of my customers emailed me and like, dang it. And then also I see one email from John Reese that says like, something like we did it or something like that. As I did what? So I click on the link to read it and I opened it up and just the day prior, they had started their launch and he kind of told the story. He said, look, we did this launch, it was selling a thousand dollar course. And the first 18 hours we sold a thousand copies, we made a million dollars in 18 hours. I remember like sitting there and like, it was kind of weird situation, like everything behind me, they just disappeared. I sat back in my chair and I was like, a million dollars in a day, in 18 hours. I was doing the math and like, like a thousand dollar course, he just, he just sell thousand. I was like, what if I create something that's thousand bucks? I sold like three a day. I gotta be a millionaire within a year. I was like, oh my gosh. And all of a sudden like the wills in my head start spinning and like all of a sudden I believed it was possible. I was like, I had belief, like this is actually, I could actually do this. And the thousand dollar month goal was stupid because like I could actually make a million dollars. I never thought it, I never even like crossed my mind, never believed it. And as soon as John had done it, I believed I could do it. And it's interesting is that after that, within a year, I almost made a million dollars. I missed, I was trying to do like an account of it and I've missed it by like, I don't know, like a hundred grand or something. So the next year I tried again, I missed it by like whatever, by third year, I hit a million dollars in a 12 month period of time. And I look back and I was like, man, if it wasn't for the fact that John did it, I wouldn't even try it because he did, I had belief that I could do it. And so I started thinking about that as we're building our culture. I was like, how do we cause belief for our people? Because they all believe that inside of phones, they can like have all their wildest dreams come true. Like if they believe that, like how would that change things? And so that time we went through, I was wondering, I was like, I wonder how many people inside of ClickFunnels actually made over a million dollars? So we went through the database and we export. We can't see all sales, we can see stripe sales and a couple other things, so we export it all. And the first initial export, I think there was 51 people that made a million dollars. I was like, are you kidding me? We made 51 millionaires so far. And that's only people we can track. So we started putting fillers out there and in the last few months we found out like, we're just shy of a hundred people that made a million dollars in ClickFunnels. That at least one funnel, multiple people have like three, four, five, multiple funnels that made a million dollars. And so as we're coming towards this event, I was like, man, we need to do something to like, because everyone realized that all their peers are doing it. Like it becomes so real, like they have so much belief in it. So we put together a trophy. I don't know if you guys have seen this yet. This is the Two Comma Club. Woo hoo. Woo hoo. So the Two Commas is Two Commas a million dollars. I was a little slow, I didn't get it at first. So one comma and a thousand, Two Commas make a million. So that's Two Comma Club, that's the name. I was like, I want a gold record and then every time someone gets a funnel, I'm gonna ship one of these things out to them. And I want everyone of our people having walls of like these things. So I went through ours, we have, it's a 13, 13 of our funnels made over a million bucks. We would get 13 of these printed for me. They're gonna be in the office here in the week or two. And then we're bringing like 90 something, these is the funnel hacking line. A lot of you guys are getting them. We're gonna be handing it out to everybody. And my goal is I want, I want to give these to people for a couple of reasons. One is they can show it on the wall. The second off, I want all my community, everyone else who's here to see that and be like, man, this is not like a rare thing. There's over a hundred people already that made over a million bucks. Like if they can do it, like I can do it. So for you guys, it's like, how do you create something? Some kind of goal, some kind of achievement, something that helps them to break the four minute mile. So first question I want to ask you guys, just think through is like, for you, what's the four minute mile for your business? Like what's that thing that, that if they thought they could do it, would change their life and probably change your life as well. Like what would that thing be? So that's the first question. And then the second thing is like, what can you guys create that gives them this target? Like the thing to be going towards. Because if there's a goal, like we have something to do, right? And the Bible says, if without a vision, the people perish, like I don't have a vision of what's possible, like I'm gonna fall away. You're gonna lose your people, but they can see it and like it's tangible. And like, man, I can see that. Like I see all these people, like we have, if you go to comicclub.com, like everyone's pictures are going on that. We're gonna have, so this is the one that will be sending to everyone. We're getting many ones made of everyone. And they're like, our hallways will be wrapped in these things. When people come to our offices, like, dang, everyone makes a million bucks and click funnels. I need you too. Like that's what I want the atmosphere and the environment people to think and to believe. And so for you guys, think about that. Like what is it you can create? In fact, that's the next workshop. So we're gonna turn the music up. What can you create? Like what's the four minute mile for your business? And what can you create that gives people like that thing? And now initially, you may not have that. Like maybe you don't have a customer who's made a million bucks yet. But like what is it that you've achieved? Okay, people are coming to you because you've achieved something. So usually, first is you, right? Like, and then what is it that they can achieve through you? Do you have something, Dave? Yeah, I was gonna say on that, we had our certified partners here last week and it was they, we brought them into the office and they're into the thing and it was amazing. Every single one of them took a picture with that plaque as basically motivation. And as we've been going through these dream, all the two comic club things, it's been fascinating for me to see how many people said that's my target, that's my goal. And so what Russell's saying there is, it's amazing how many people as soon as they see it, that's their goal. I can't tell you how many people have this now up on their vision wall, their dream boards, whatever they call it. It's just crazy, just having something tangible that they can see and just change everybody's life. It's really cool. We used to have a big sales team of 60 guys here for a while and what's interesting is we would try to bribe these guys with everything. Like, we'll give you cash, we'll give you, like we had some fun things. Like one time we brought in a whole bunch of cash, like one, mostly one, but in five, tens of a couple hundred dollar bills. And then we like laid them over the whole ground and then we wrapped everybody in tape. And then based on how many clothes they did that week, how long they got to roll around on the floor and they keep whatever stuck to them, which was super fun, right? That was cool. But I don't know why he told you that story other than it was really fun. And we had all these videos of people rolling around in cash and then it was really cool. But outside of that, cash never motivated any of our salespeople. Like a thousand bucks for every of these, whatever, like that never motivated people. We started doing stupid stuff like, hey, the new whatever thing, like gave them like the new beats by Dre headphones. Whoever gets most sales this week will do it. People will go nuts for like things like that. Our affiliate contests, like when we give away Ferraris, guess what happens? One person, maybe two go excited. I give away like the Yeti mics, guess who gets excited? For somebody we had over a hundred and something new affiliates who never promote our stuff, go crazy because they wanted a Yeti mic like Russell's. Like it's something about that like that tangible thing that gets people excited more than cash even. And so if you can create that that gives them like, that's what I want, that's the goal, that's what I'm going towards, then they've got something they're moving towards. Otherwise they're gonna come in, buy your thing and then they're gonna leave and go to the next person to plug into. Like if you're the one that's got like, this is where we're all going collectively to community, then everybody will follow and it comes back to this whole culture building stuff. Cool? All right, so stay two minutes and figure out what's your four minute mile and then what can you create to do it? So two minutes, we'll turn music on and just walk and talk, find someone and tell them, explain to them and just create some four minute mile breakers. All right, real quick, Joseph's got a question about this. All right. Okay, so I kind of get the idea when it comes to that monetary gain, you get some physical thing or losing weight, like you lose 10 pounds, you get a trophy or some sort. How can you do it in the intangible? Like I do, my main clients are low self-esteem, don't believe they're good enough, so their result will be, I actually do love myself, I am worthy, I am good enough, but how can you convert that into? Who is an intangible of their own they wanna share, just to give examples of one. Yeah, throw the box. So my program is really about having the courage to do something and I call it a bad ass permission slip. So my thought was to give like a necklace or a dog tag or a key or a permission slip that they've gone through the process to give themselves permission to move forward. So if you can symbolize it with something that shows strength or freedom or whatever, then you can give it a similar kind of thing. It's cool, and that's what I wanna share. You have that are specifically? Yeah, sure. For me, I was trying to market the aesthetics and basically we're thinking about, trying to market to consumers, so basically, after getting the treatment, they're gonna look a lot better. We'll actually go buy a full page ad, run a photo shoot of them, put it on the ad and say, hey look, this lady went from having a turkey chin and now it's gone, is she so good looking? She's on this magazine or whatever. Exactly, they say, hey, if that lady can do it, certainly it's tokenized. I think I'm struggling with the same thing that Joseph is, is that I've like, okay, cool, you've had seven difficult conversations, like how do you get there? But as Julie was sharing, I think I thought of like, recovery programs, they give you chips for like certain amount of days, and so my program's called Life On Purpose, so it's like, well, you've been living your life on purpose for 30 days, for six months, for a year, like some kind of a thing where it's like, now they're in continuity where it's like, well, I can't leave, because then I'll stop living my life on purpose. You're not on purpose anymore, that's perfect. I think that sometimes having testimonial, not sometimes, every time having testimonials can be really, really motivating and help people see their million dollar idea. Sometimes, especially in your guys' niches, it can be hard to get those testimonials though, because people might feel nervous sharing it, but what I've done in the past that works really well is I'll say, anybody who does my program and finishes the full 12 weeks, I used to do it, then I would do it to everybody, but I'd say, if you finish the 12 weeks and send in either a written testimony with a picture or a video testimonial, they need to get a 20 minute Skype session with me. And so then people are like, oh my gosh, I should get to talk with Natalie, you know? And now it's different, or else I have to choose people out of the winners, but that's a good way to like get those testimonials from people too. Awesome, very cool. All right, how many of you guys are excited about your four minute mile? Boom, perfect. Nice. Cool. This is something that obviously we're just introducing, but I know for me personally, like what drove me for the last decade, and I think that like the more you create that, and some of these, while I see you guys, we might have to think outside of just in this room, but thinking through that, like what do they want to achieve and create in the belief? Because people believe they'll move, they believe they'll buy, when they believe like they'll follow, but if they don't believe, that's when they fall away. Everything we're trying to do is, how do we create belief? How do we create belief? I mean, and this comes deep when we get into the story telling stuff tomorrow. Like it's telling stories to cause belief. Like that if nothing else, you get from this entire experience, like how do we cause belief in the minds of our customers? That's the key. That's what gets them to be with you.
